tool_args.label: NYCT, SIR and MTA Bus Financial & Ridership Report, October 2022

payload.title: NYCT, SIR and MTA Bus Financial & Ridership Report, October 2022
payload.description: Financial and Ridership Reports for New York City Transit, Staten Island Railway, and MTA Bus Company for October 2022

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: MTA New York City Transit operating agency
MTA Staten Island Railway operating agency
MTA Bus Company operating agency
Total revenues of $3,736 billion were $114.3 million (3.0%) lower than the Forecast
Total ridership was 1,124.0 million, which exceeded the Forecast by 3.2 million (0.3%)
Total expenses of $10.218 billion including non-cash liabilities were $240.1 million (2.3%) lower than the Forecast
At the end of October, total headcount was 45,904, which was 2,724 lower than the Forecast of 48,628
The year-to-date October Farebox Operating Ratio was 33.0%, which is above the Forecast by 1.5 percentage points
The year-to-date October Cost per Passenger was $6.73, which is lower than the Forecast by $0.08 per passenger
The year-to-date October Revenue per Passenger was $2.22, which was higher than the Forecast by $0.08 per passenger

payload.description: Total revenues of $5.538 million were $5.390 million (49.3%) lower than the Mid-Year Forecast
Total ridership was 1.648 million, 25.3% lower than Forecast
Total expenses of $69.031 million including non-cash liabilities were $7.162 million (9.4%) lower than the Forecast
At the end of October, total headcount was 349, which was 48 lower than the Forecast of 397
Total revenues of $152.5 million were $9.0 million (6.2%) favorable to the Mid-Year Forecast
Total ridership was 68.8 million, 7.0% greater than Forecast
Total expenses of $740.6 million including non-cash liabilities were $178.2 million (19.4%) lower than the Forecast
At the end of October, total headcount was 3,776, which was 239 lower than the Forecast of 4,014
The year-to-date October Farebox Operating Ratio was 5.8%, which is lower than the Forecast by 1.6 percentage points
The year-to-date October Cost per Passenger was $34.35, which is higher than the Forecast by $7.01 per passenger
The year-to-date October Revenue per Passenger was $2.00, which was lower than Forecast by $0.03 per passenger

tool_args.label: MTA Bus YTD Farebox Operating Ratio

payload.description: The year-to-date October Farebox Operating Ratio was 19.3%, which is higher than the Forecast by 3.7 percentage points

tool_args.label: MTA Bus YTD Cost per Passenger

payload.description: The year-to-date October Cost per Passenger was $10.12, which is lower than the forecast by $1.96

tool_args.label: MTA Bus YTD Revenue per Passenger

payload.description: The year-to-date October Revenue per passenger was $1.96, which was higher than the Forecast by $0.07 per passenger

tool_args.label: Jaibala Patel prepared the financial report

payload.description: Jaibala Patel, Deputy Chief Financial Officer, MTA, prepared the Financial and Ridership Reports

tool_args.label: NYCT Overtime Unfavorable Due to Vacancy Coverage

payload.claim_text: Overtime was $199.3 million (48.9%) unfavorable due primarily to vacancy / absentee coverage needs and additional maintenance requirements
payload.subject: NYCT Non-Reimbursable Overtime

tool_args.label: NYCT Payroll Favorable Due to Vacancies

payload.claim_text: Payroll was $120.0 million (4.0%) favorable primarily due to the existence of 2,724 vacancies
payload.subject: NYCT Non-Reimbursable Payroll

tool_args.label: NYCT Largest Vacancies in Subways Maintenance

payload.claim_text: The largest number of vacancies were in Subways Maintenance (1,468 positions) and Subways Operations (429 positions)
payload.subject: NYCT Staffing

payload.claim_text: The largest number of vacancies were in maintenance (25 positions). 79% of the vacancies were in the operational hourlies category.
payload.subject: SIR Staffing

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Largest Vacancies in Maintenance

payload.claim_text: The largest number of vacancies were in maintenance (229 positions). 58% percent of the vacancies are in the operational hourlies category.
payload.subject: MTA Bus Company Staffing

tool_args.label: Results are preliminary and subject to adjustment

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.
